OPPOSING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS A COURTESY COPY OF THE MOTION AND REPLY. MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-15-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 8, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR CAUSES OF ACTION 4, 5, AND 6. (PLAINTIFF STATES IN OPPOSITION THAT SHE INTENDS TO AMEND THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION.) THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED FOR CAUSE OF ACTION 7. THE CLRA CLAIM (CAUSE OF ACTION 5) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SERVICE OF THE NOTICE IN THE COMPLAINT AND TO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATION MORE CLEARLY IN THE COMPLAINT. THE UCL CLAIM (CAUSE OF ACTION 6) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD PARTICULAR FACTS REGARDING HOW DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS UNFAIR, UNLAWFUL, OR FRAUDULENT. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS BEYOND RESTATING THE BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM (CAUSE OF ACTION 7) IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED ALL THE ELEMENTS REQUIRED FOR NEGLIGENCE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.
ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND OVERRULING IN PART DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER

MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND OPERATIVE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 18, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE OVERRULED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAUSE OF ACTION APPEARS TO BE IMPROPERLY SEEKING TORT DAMAGES AND WHILE PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO CONTRACTUAL DAMAGES, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. SEE CAREAU & CO. V. SECURITY PACIFIC BUSINESS CREDIT, INC. (1990), 222 CAL. APP. 3D 1371, 1395. THE DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE CLRA IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPHS 8, 37-38, AND 42 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO DELIVER THE PROMISED WARRANTY. THOSE ALLEGATIONS SUPPORT THE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF'S CLRA NOTICE IS TIMELY PER MORGAN V. AT & T WIRELESS SERVICES, INC. (2009), 177 CAL. APP. 4TH 1235, 1261. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL. PARAGRAPHS 8, 37-38, 42, AND 46C SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 30, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE MOVING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF ITS MOVING PAPERS WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)
LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, IS ADVANCED FROM OCT-30-2014 TO OCT-14-2014, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. (D302)
RE-NOTICE OF HEARING AND FORMAL DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION STATE CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, RESPECTIVELY, AND ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) ALLEGES THAT A FIRE DESTROYED PLAINTIFF'S CAR, CAUSING LOSS TO PLAINTIFF. (SEE, E.G., SAC AT 17.) THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE AND THEREFORE IT CANNOT BE DETERMINED BASED ON THE PLEADINGS WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S POLICY INCLUDED COVERAGE OF THE LOSS. COURT INSTRUCTS ATTORNEY MS. NGUYEN, AS COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY, TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORMAL ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED.(302/MJM)

Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Advanced From Oct-30-14 To Oct-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney Thu Nguyen appearing via CourtCall for Plaintiff Linda Davidson.
Attorney Brandon Finn appearing via CourtCall for Defendant Euromotors, Inc.
Attorney Matt Lanhardt present for Defendant CSAA Insurance Exchange.

Court informs parties that there is not a court reporter present for today's hearing, and offers parties a continuance. Parties indicate that they would like to proceed today, and waive reporting of today's hearing by a court reporter.

Argument presented by counsel. Matter submitted. Court adopts its tentative ruling.
Defendant CSAA Insurance Exchange's Demurrer to 2nd Amended Complaint is OVERRULED. The Ninth and Tenth Causes of Action state claims for breach of contract and breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, respectively, and are not uncertain. The Second Amended Complaint (SAC) alleges that a fire destroyed Plaintiff's car, causing loss to Plaintiff. (See, e.g., SAC at 17.) The cause of the fire is a factual issue and therefore it cannot be determined based on the pleadings whether Plaintiff's policy included coverage of the loss. Court instructs attorney Ms. Nguyen, as counsel for the prevailing party, to prepare and submit a proposed formal order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: S. Kane; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-16-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-12-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph Wheeler, Esq. of Universal, Shannon & Wheeler, LLP, Tel. 916.780.4050, for defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
Thu Nguyen, Esq. of Parr Law Group, Tel. 408.267.4500, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, modified as follows: The Court sustains the demurrer to the 4th cause of action for breach of the implied covenant without leave to amend. The cause of action appears to be improperly seeking tort damages and while plaintiff is entitled to contractual damages, the cause of action is duplicative of the breach of contract claim. See Careau & Co. v. Security Pacific Business Credit, Inc. (1990), 222 Cal. App. 3d 1371, 1395. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for violation of the CLRA is overruled. Paragraphs 8, 37-38, and 42 of the amended complaint allege that defendant failed to deliver the promised warranty. Those allegations support the claim. Plaintiff's CLRA notice is timely per Morgan v. AT & T Wireless Services, Inc. (2009), 177 Cal. App. 4th 1235, 1261. The Court overrules the demurrer to the 6th cause of action for violation of the UCL. Paragraphs 8, 37-38, 42, and 46c sufficiently plead an unfair business practice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-01-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for causes of action 4, 5, and 6. (Plaintiff states in opposition that she intends to amend the 4th and 5th causes of action.) The demurrer is overruled for cause of action 7. The CLRA claim (cause of action 5) is sustained with leave to amend to plead service of the notice in the complaint and to plead misrepresentation more clearly in the complaint. The UCL claim (cause of action 6) is sustained with leave to amend to plead particular facts regarding how Defendant's conduct was unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent. Plaintiff must plead facts beyond restating the breach of contract. The Negligence claim (cause of action 7) is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged all the elements required for negligence. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Plaintiff's complaint is granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
